data.overview:
**DEMO engagement — illustrative as-is map of the UC San Diego Staff Performance Appraisal (SPA) process.** This is sample data created to demonstrate the application end to end; names and figures are illustrative.

## What this engagement maps

UC San Diego requires every career staff member to receive a written performance appraisal at least once a year. The process is coordinated through the online Staff Performance Appraisal (SPA) system (spa.ucsd.edu) and follows campus-wide timelines. The appraisal cycle runs **May 1 – April 30**; all appraisals must be completed within 45 calendar days after the cycle ends, with a campus-wide deadline of **close of business Monday, June 15, 2026**.

## Scope

From **setting expectations** (job-description review and goal-setting at the start of the cycle) through the **signed appraisal filed in the employee's personnel file**. Mapped at the size of the appraisal lifecycle itself, not the whole employee lifecycle.

## The Layer 1 discipline

Layer 1 produces the lifecycle map: a written, agreed account of how the service actually runs — its stages, points of contact, handoffs, decisions, and where it has friction. **It describes the service as it is and proposes no fixes.** Naming AI opportunities is Layer 2's job; only two things carry forward — the list of decisions and the Friction Register.

The six artifacts here are not sequential phases. They are parallel lenses on the same whole service: the **journey map** (the employee's experience) and the **service blueprint** (the operations behind it) share one set of stages and line up stage for stage; the **process documentation** is the detail beneath; the **interview guide** is the shared raw source; the **friction register** logs evidence gathered across all of them; and the **validation packet** integrates the whole and records sign-off.

## How AI was used

AI tagged interview notes against a shared label set, drafted first cuts of the journey and blueprint, and grouped related friction — each suggestion shown with the words that prompted it. A person confirmed or rejected every item. See the AI decision log on the Validation screen.
